title: Xenial Korean Pork Belly Stir-Fry
description: Citrusy, chewy pork belly with smoked paprika and nuka-pickle, a lazy Sunday Korean dish that stays keto-friendly. This bokkeum is quick, flavorful, and perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add pork belly slices and sear until browned on both sides, about 3-4 minutes per side.
Sprinkle smoked paprika, garlic, and ginger over the pork and stir to coat.
Pour soy sauce and add chopped nuka-pickle, cooking until the pork is almost fully cooked, about 5 minutes.
Reduce heat to medium and stir in sesame seeds, letting them toast for 30 seconds.
Add sliced scallions, cooking for an additional minute until softened.
Remove from heat and let the mixture rest for 2 minutes so the flavors meld.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with extra scallions or a drizzle of sesame oil.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
